title: Backlog parser + PBI counts in bee_status and preamble + optional pbi field on cells
action: Three mechanical additions. (1) New templates/lib/backlog.mjs exporting readBacklogCounts(root): parse docs/backlog.md markdown table rows, read ONLY the Status column tokens (exact statuses: proposed, in-flight, done — the fixed enum per D6), return {proposed, inFlight, done, total} or null when the file is missing. Tolerance rules: locate the Status column by header name (case-insensitive) so extra columns and reordering survive; strip bold/inline markup from cells before matching; a row whose status is not one of the three tokens is skipped, never a throw; duplicate IDs are counted honestly (each row counts — duplicate detection is grooming prose, not parser logic). (2) bee_status.mjs includes a pbi field in --json output ({proposed, in_flight, done} or null) and one human line when non-null; buildSessionPreamble in inject.mjs gains exactly one line 'PBI: N done / N in-flight / N proposed' appended inside the Project map section when readBacklogCounts returns non-null (section cap becomes 2-5 lines with this line — update the existing cap comment (inject.mjs ~line 50) and BOTH existing assertions: test_lib.mjs ~line 540 (section.length === 4 becomes the 5-line max case) and ~line 544 (the !/PBI/ slice-4-boundary assertion is repurposed: it now asserts the PBI line absent only when docs/backlog.md is missing); per D10 the line appears only when docs/backlog.md exists — and it appends in BOTH branches of the Project map section, warning case included: heading + warning + PBI = 3 lines is valid). (3) templates/lib/cells.mjs: accept an optional pbi string field on cell add — persisted verbatim, NO validation coupling (missing/stale reference is a grooming find, not a cap blocker, per D9); reject non-string values only. Tests in templates/tests/test_lib.mjs: counts on a well-formed backlog fixture; missing file -> null and no PBI line in preamble; extra columns + bold text tolerated; malformed row skipped without throw; duplicate ids counted; cells add with pbi persists, cap ignores it; preamble line format exact. Export the status enum as a constant from backlog.mjs and add a drift-guard assertion for it in the tests (same pattern as COMMAND_KEYS, per CONTEXT Established Patterns). Fixtures under the shared test root clean up in try/finally.

truths: readBacklogCounts returns null when docs/backlog.md is absent and the preamble omits the PBI line
A malformed or unknown-status row is skipped without throwing and the remaining rows still count
A cell with a pbi field caps exactly like one without it (no validation coupling)
The preamble PBI line appears only when backlog.md exists and follows the exact format PBI: N done / N in-flight / N proposed

artifacts.substantive: exports readBacklogCounts with header-located Status column parsing, no TODO stubs

key_links: inject.mjs imports readBacklogCounts from ./backlog.mjs; bee_status.mjs consumes the same function - one parser, no duplicated logic
prohibitions: No status transitions, no writes to docs/backlog.md from any helper (prose-ruled per D7 - the parser only reads)
No new cap/claim refusal paths from the pbi field (D9)
No changes to buildPromptReminder or inject cache logic

trace.outcome: New templates/lib/backlog.mjs parses docs/backlog.md Status column (D6 enum proposed/in-flight/done, tolerant of absence/extra columns/bold/malformed, honest duplicate counts); bee_status --json+text and buildSessionPreamble surface a PBI line only when backlog.md exists; cells accept an optional pbi string (D9, no validation coupling). One shared parser, no writes (D7).
trace.verification_evidence:
verification_evidence — harness10-6

Behavior change: bee_status --json + text and the session preamble now surface a
PBI line derived from docs/backlog.md; cells accept an optional pbi field.

Tests inspected (existing, repurposed):
- test_lib.mjs "both map files exist" case: assertion section.length===4 became a
  two-phase case — 4 lines without backlog.md, 5-line max WITH the PBI line; the
  !/PBI/ slice-4-boundary assertion repurposed to "no PBI line when backlog absent".

Tests added:
- readBacklogCounts: null on absent file (+ no preamble PBI line); counts a
  well-formed backlog; tolerates extra/reordered columns + bold/code markup;
  skips malformed/unknown-status rows without throwing; counts duplicate IDs
  honestly; BACKLOG_STATUSES drift guard (export == source literal, COMMAND_KEYS
  pattern).
- cells: optional pbi persists on add and survives cap untouched (no validation
  coupling); non-string pbi rejected; absent pbi stays absent.

Red-failure evidence: before wiring, importing readBacklogCounts/BACKLOG_STATUSES
from ../lib/backlog.mjs would fail module resolution (file did not exist); the new
assertions reference the exported symbols, so the suite could not pass until the
parser and wiring landed.

Verification run (exact cell verify command):
  node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs
Result: test_lib.mjs -> "59 passed, 0 failed" (exit 0); test_onboard_bee.mjs ->
"PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0" (exit 0).

End-to-end drive (real template code, not just tests):
- Repo root (no docs/backlog.md): bee_status --json -> "pbi": null; no PBI text line.
- Fixture with backlog.md (1 done / 1 in-flight / 2 proposed):
  * JSON pbi = {"proposed":2,"in_flight":1,"done":1}
  * text line: "PBI: 1 done / 1 in-flight / 2 proposed"
  * preamble Project map (warning branch): heading + warning + "- PBI: 1 done / 1 in-flight / 2 proposed" (3 lines, within the 2–5 cap).
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 59 passed, 0 failed (exit 0). test_onboard_bee.mjs: PASS - failures: 0, skipped: 0 (exit 0).
